question_text: A student sets up two circuits with identical 12V power supplies. Circuit A has a 4Ω resistor, and Circuit B has two 4Ω resistors in series. The student measures the current in each circuit. Which statement correctly compares the currents and explains why?

options.label: A
options.text: Circuit A has 3A and Circuit B has 3A because the voltage is the same in both

options.label: B
options.text: Circuit A has 3A and Circuit B has 1.5A because Circuit B has twice the total resistance

options.label: C
options.text: Circuit A has 1.5A and Circuit B has 3A because series circuits increase current

options.label: D
options.text: Both circuits have the same current because they use the same power supply

explanation: Using Ohm's Law (V = IR), Circuit A: I = 12V ÷ 4Ω = 3A. Circuit B: total resistance = 4Ω + 4Ω = 8Ω, so I = 12V ÷ 8Ω = 1.5A. When resistors are added in series, total resistance increases, so current decreases.
